faqs.question: How do I know if I have a clothes moth infestation in my Jamaica home?
faqs.answer: Signs of clothes moth infestation include small irregular holes in wool, silk, or cashmere clothing, webbing in closet corners, small cream-colored larvae on fabrics, and adult moths flying near wardrobes. You may also notice damage to carpets, upholstery, or stored blankets. Our Jamaica technicians can perform a thorough inspection to confirm the presence of clothes moths.

faqs.question: What does clothes moth treatment cost in Jamaica, Queens?
faqs.answer: Clothes moth control services in Jamaica typically range from $550 to $5,000, depending on the severity of the infestation, the size of your property, and the number of affected areas. Minor closet infestations cost less, while whole-home treatments for severe cases require more extensive service. We provide free inspections and detailed quotes for all Jamaica residents.

faqs.question: How long does clothes moth treatment take to work?
faqs.answer: Most treatments begin working immediately, with adult moths and larvae eliminated within the first few days. Complete eradication typically requires 2-4 weeks as eggs hatch and are exposed to treatment residuals. Follow-up visits may be necessary for severe infestations to ensure all life stages are eliminated and your Jamaica home remains moth-free.

faqs.question: How can I prevent clothes moths from returning to my Jamaica home?
faqs.answer: Prevention includes regularly cleaning closets and storage areas, dry cleaning or washing woolens before storage, using cedar blocks or lavender sachets as natural deterrents, vacuuming carpets and upholstery frequently, and storing off-season clothing in sealed containers. Our Jamaica team provides detailed prevention guidance and offers ongoing monitoring services to keep your home protected year-round.
